title: Existence of interior discount factor where decreasing and increasing value trajectories coincide
background_latex:
Let $K=\{1,\dots,k\}$ be a finite state space and let $M$ be an irreducible stochastic matrix on $K$ with unique invariant distribution $\pi_M\in\Delta(K)$. In a Markovian persuasion game, the sender observes the current state $X_n\in K$ of the Markov chain in real time and sends a signal; the receiver chooses an action based on his posterior belief, yielding a stage payoff to the sender. For each discount factor $\delta\in[0,1)$ and prior $q\in\Delta(K)$, let $v_\delta(q)$ denote the sender's optimal expected $(1-\delta)$-normalized $\delta$-discounted payoff.

Let $e_\ell\in\Delta(K)$ denote the Dirac belief on state $\ell\in K$ (i.e., $e_\ell(\ell)=1$). Define two functions of the discount factor $\delta$:
\[
\Phi(\delta):=v_\delta(\pi_M),\qquad \Psi(\delta):=\sum_{\ell\in K} \pi_M(\ell)\, v_\delta(e_\ell).
\]
For irreducible $M$, the paper establishes that $\Phi$ is non-increasing in $\delta$ (when the prior is invariant) and that $\Psi$ is non-decreasing in $\delta$ (as a particular instance of an increasing weighted-average trajectory). Moreover, for each fixed $\delta$, concavity of $v_\delta(\cdot)$ implies $\Phi(\delta)\ge \Psi(\delta)$.

Define
\[
\delta_0:=\inf\{\delta\in[0,1):\Phi(\delta)=\Psi(\delta)\},
\]
with the convention that the set may be empty (equivalently, $\Phi(\delta)>\Psi(\delta)$ for all $\delta\in[0,1)$). The paper notes that if equality holds at some $\delta_0$, then both $\Phi$ and $\Psi$ must be constant on $[\delta_0,1)$; hence the qualitative possibility of an equality point $\delta_0\in(0,1)$ is a distinct regime from equality only at $\delta=1$ (in the limit) or no equality at all.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine whether there exists a Markovian persuasion game with finite state space $K$ and irreducible transition matrix $M$ such that the associated functions
\[
\Phi(\delta)=v_\delta(\pi_M),\qquad \Psi(\delta)=\sum_{\ell\in K} \pi_M(\ell)\, v_\delta(e_\ell)
\]
satisfy
\[
\Phi(\delta)>\Psi(\delta)\ \text{for all }\delta\in[0,\delta_0),\qquad \Phi(\delta)=\Psi(\delta)\ \text{for all }\delta\in[\delta_0,1)
\]
for some \emph{interior} point $\delta_0\in(0,1)$ (equivalently, whether equality can first occur strictly before $\delta=1$).
latex:

Determine whether there exists a Markovian persuasion game with finite state space $K$ and irreducible transition matrix $M$ such that the associated functions
\[
\Phi(\delta)=v_\delta(\pi_M),\qquad \Psi(\delta)=\sum_{\ell\in K} \pi_M(\ell)\, v_\delta(e_\ell)

location_in_paper: Section 3.2.1 ("On the Analytic Behavior of Two Trajectories"), page 12 (PDF pagination shown as 12/51).
open_problem_quote: Nevertheless, the existence of an example for Case B in which \(0 < \delta_0 < 1\) (where \(\delta_0\) was described in Corollary 1) remains an open question.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The open question asks for existence of a constructed instance (or proof of impossibility) producing an interior crossing point $\delta_0$. One can algorithmically search over small state spaces and parametrized payoff functions (e.g., piecewise-linear $u$ induced by receiver best responses) and numerically approximate $v_\delta$ via dynamic programming, but the space of games is large and exact verification of an interior equality point is delicate due to numerical error.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate instances can be represented by a small Markov matrix $M$ and payoff tables $V,W$ (or directly by an induced stage-value function $u$ consistent with some receiver action rule). Fitness can be computed by numerically solving for $v_\delta(\cdot)$ on a grid of $\delta$ values and scoring how close the trajectory exhibits a first equality $\Phi=\Psi$ at an interior $\delta_0$ with persistence thereafter. An evolutionary loop can iteratively mutate $(M,V,W)$ and refine toward the desired crossing pattern.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper (Shaiderman, 2025) isolates two canonical discount-factor trajectories in Markovian persuasion when the Markov chain is irreducible: the invariant-prior value \(\Phi(\delta)=v_\delta(\pi_M)\), shown to be non-increasing in \(\delta\), and the Dirac-weighted average \(\Psi(\delta)=\sum_{\ell}\pi_M(\ell)v_\delta(e_\ell)\), known from Lehrer and Shaiderman (2025, stochastic revelations) to be non-decreasing. Concavity of \(v_\delta(\cdot)\) yields \(\Phi(\delta)\ge \Psi(\delta)\) for each \(\delta\). A key structural corollary is that if equality \(\Phi(\delta_0)=\Psi(\delta_0)\) holds at some \(\delta_0<1\), then both trajectories must be constant on \([\delta_0,1)\), so the question becomes whether one can realize a genuine “kink” regime where strict inequality holds for \(\delta<\delta_0\) but equality holds for all larger \(\delta\).

Existing results and examples in Shaiderman (2025) and earlier work show that several qualitative regimes are feasible: (i) strict separation \(\Phi>\Psi\) for all \(\delta\in[0,1)\) (Case A in the paper’s Figure 1) can occur even with ergodic \(M\); (ii) non-meeting can occur in periodic settings (Case C). However, no construction is currently known that forces first equality at an interior \(\delta_0\in(0,1)\) (Case B with \(\delta_0>0\)), and no impossibility theorem rules it out. The monotonicity-through-information-design techniques (memory erasures for \(\Phi\), stochastic revelations for \(\Psi\)) explain why any interior meeting would correspond to a region where the optimal policy effectively becomes \(\delta\)-independent and simultaneously saturates the concavity bound \(v_\delta(\pi_M)=\sum_\ell \pi_M(\ell)v_\delta(e_\ell)\).

Promising directions suggested by the current state of the art include: (a) characterizing when Jensen-tightness \(v_\delta(\pi_M)=\mathbb{E}[v_\delta(p)]\) can hold for the particular split induced by \(\pi_M=\sum_\ell \pi_M(\ell)e_\ell\), which would require \(v_\delta\) to be affine on the simplex region spanned by the \(e_\ell\) relevant under the optimal stationary policy; (b) exploiting semi-algebraicity/piecewise-linearity phenomena of concavifications in finite-state models to engineer a phase transition in the optimal split at a threshold \(\delta_0\); and (c) translating the question to the related Markov chain games framework (Renault, 2006) where additional strategic structure might make such a threshold easier to construct or to preclude.

sources.title: Markovian persuasion
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes existence of an asymptotic value v_∞ under ergodicity and develops the dynamic programming/MDP approach for Markovian persuasion; provides examples and structural results used in later monotonicity/rate arguments.
sources.connection: Provides the baseline model and convergence framework in which Φ(δ) and Ψ(δ) are compared; the open question about an interior equality point δ0 is posed relative to the monotonicity phenomena developed across this line of work.

sources.title: Markovian persuasion with stochastic revelations
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves existence of increasing weighted-average trajectories in δ (including Ψ(δ)=∑_ℓ π_M(ℓ)v_δ(e_ℓ) non-decreasing) via an information-design coupling with stochastic revelations.
sources.connection: Supplies the monotone increasing counterpart Ψ(δ) used to define δ0 and to deduce that if Φ(δ0)=Ψ(δ0) then both trajectories are constant on [δ0,1); thus it is central to the interior-δ0 question.

sources.title: On the Monotonicity and Rate of Convergence of the Markovian Persuasion Value
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows Φ(δ)=v_δ(π_M) is non-increasing for invariant priors; combines with increasing Ψ(δ) to bound convergence rate; exhibits Cases A/C and leaves open existence of Case B with interior δ0.
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open problem; it proves the structural constraint that any interior equality point forces constancy on [δ0,1), sharpening the question to existence of such a regime.

sources.title: The value of Markov chain games with lack of information on one side
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces Markov chain games and proves existence/structure of discounted values in dynamic incomplete-information settings; provides the related framework extended in Shaiderman (2025).
sources.connection: While not directly addressing Φ vs Ψ, it supplies the broader dynamic-information and MDP machinery; analogous monotonicity questions for invariant priors motivate the persuasion setting and may offer construction ideas/counterexamples.

sources.title: Optimal dynamic information provision
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops dynamic information provision with Markovian state evolution, characterizing optimal policies via concavification/dynamic programming in related models.
sources.connection: Provides foundational techniques for analyzing v_δ(q) as a concave value function and may inform attempts to construct examples where Φ and Ψ coincide on a tail interval.

sources.title: Beeps

sources.theorem_or_result: Studies a dynamic persuasion/information design model with evolving states and derives optimal disclosure structures in a stylized setting.
sources.connection: Offers alternative constructions and intuition for dynamic persuasion values; could inspire payoff/transition designs that might generate an interior δ0 regime, though no direct result on Φ vs Ψ.
